questions.stem: You need to rollback a VPC configuration change. The problematic commit is `a1b2c3d`. What is the CORRECT command sequence?

options.text: git revert a1b2c3d && git push origin main
kubectl delete vpc vpc-prod && kubectl apply -f vpc-prod-backup.yaml
git checkout a1b2c3d~1 && git push origin main

questions.stem: You need to delete VPC `prod-vpc`, which has 3 VPCAttachments and 1 VPCPeering. What is the CORRECT deletion order?

options.text: Delete VPC → Delete VPCAttachments → Delete VPCPeering
Delete VPCAttachments → Delete VPCPeering → Delete VPC
Delete VPCPeering → Delete VPC → Delete VPCAttachments
Delete all resources simultaneously with kubectl delete vpc,vpcattachment,vpcpeering --all

questions.stem: You deleted VPC `test-vpc` 15 minutes ago but it's still showing 'Terminating' status with finalizers present. What should you do FIRST?

options.text: Immediately remove finalizers with kubectl patch
Check if VPCAttachments still exist and delete them

questions.stem: ArgoCD sync failed with 'invalid YAML'. Your rollback commit is in Git but cluster state hasn't changed. What is the NEXT step?

options.text: Delete the Git commit and start over
Validate YAML locally with kubectl apply --dry-run, fix errors, and commit the fix
Force ArgoCD to sync with --force flag
Manually apply YAML with kubectl, bypassing ArgoCD
